metadata_information.title: How private investment reacts to changing macroeconomic conditions : the case of Chile in the 1980s
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: How private investment reacts to changing macroeconomic conditions : the case of Chile in the 1980s

document_description.abstract: This paper simulates a model of joint determination of private investment spending, aggregate investment profitability and the level of GDP for Chile. It addresses the following issues: (a) sharp cycles in economic activity - the boom of 1980-81, the steep recession of 1982-83, and the recovery afterward which increased the volatility of aggregate demand and discouraged investment; (b) sharp swings in the real exchange rate and real interest rates in the last decade which increased the variance of profitability causing an adverse effect on investment; (c) high real interest rates which restrained private investment; and (d) real appreciation and depreciation.
